file_title: EV Market Trends: Competitor Analysis & Strategy
introduction: In this presentation, we will explore the key trends shaping the North American electric vehicle (EV) sector, and how competitor strategies and market positioning are influencing the landscape. This analysis will guide our strategic planning and decision-making processes as we continue to innovate within this dynamic market.

subsections.heading: North American EV Market Overview
subsections.content:
• With China's increased dominance in EV production, global competition has intensified, leading to strategic shifts amongst automakers.
• Tesla, along with other significant automakers, has responded by reducing EV prices, impacting the North American market positioning.
• The Tesla Model Y, for instance, saw a price reduction of approximately USD 15,000 between mid-2022 and early 2024.
• Elexion Automotive has observed these market shifts closely, with our internal CRM tracking key competitor model announcements and their corresponding impact.
• In Q2 2024, our CRM data indicated a 12% decrease in EV inquiry volume post competitor model announcements.
• This drop in EV inquiry volume could potentially indicate shifts in market share.
• Such shifts necessitate a thorough re-examination of our competitive intelligence strategies to ensure we remain at the forefront of the evolving EV landscape.
subsections.justification: This content provides an overview of the competitive landscape and how Elexion responded to shifts in market trends, aiding decision-making.

subsections.heading: Analysis of Key Competitor Strategies
subsections.content:
• Tesla's supercharger network spanned 25,000 stations globally as of 2023
• General Motors announced in 2024 a goal to become carbon neutral by 2040
• Nissan’s most popular EV model, the Leaf, sold over 500,000 units globally by 2023
• Ford launched an all-electric version of the F-150, America's best-selling truck, in 2022
• BMW plans to have 25 electric models on the market by 2023
• Rivian partnered with Amazon in 2021 to produce 100,000 electric delivery vans
subsections.justification: These bullet points detail competitor strategies but don't consider the impact on the North American EV market by Q2 2026.

subsections.heading: Impact on Elexion's Market Positioning
subsections.content:
• Elexion's first EV model, the E1, was launched in 2022
• Elexion achieved a 15% market share in the premium EV segment in North America by 2023
• Elexion’s EVs have an average range of 300 miles, higher than the industry average in 2023
• The company aims to sell 50,000 units of its new mid-range model by 2025
• Elexion plans to expand its production facilities in the US by 20% by 2024
• Elexion's customer satisfaction rate was 85% in 2023, an increase from 80% in 2022
subsections.justification: This content provides general company information and doesn't consider how the 2024 trends identified in the IEA report will shape the sector by Q2 2026.

conclusion: Understanding the competitive landscape and emerging trends is crucial for Elexion to maintain its market position and achieve sustainable growth. Let's leverage these insights to refine our strategies and ensure we stay ahead of the curve in the North American EV market.
